item.title: FirstFT: Donald Trump backs off threat to strike Iranian energy infrastructure
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Berkshire Hathaway expands bet on Japan, and Indonesia cracks down on resource companies

item.title: Apollo caps investor withdrawals from flagship private credit fund
item.description: Investor uncertainty intensifies as AI impacts on the enterprise software industry emerge

item.title: Estée Lauder nears deal to combine with Jean Paul Gaultier owner Puig
item.description: Tie-up between US and Spanish groups would create a $40bn beauty giant

item.title: Italy’s mail-to-telecoms national champion gets a thumbs down
item.description: Poste Italiane’s €10.8bn bid looks unenticing for Telecom Italia’s investors — it should try harder to bring them onside

item.title: Starmer says UK government ‘finalising’ defence plan, but no date yet
item.description: PM blames ‘over-commitment and underfunding’ by the previous government for delays to military’s 10-year spending blueprint

item.title: US to pay Total $1bn to switch from wind to oil and gas development
item.description: Deal comes as Donald Trump faces pressure to limit energy price increases triggered by Iran war

item.title: Musk verdict suggests US shareholders are stepping into the regulatory void
item.description: The ruling against him may suggest the judicial system is leaning into its expanded role in securities law enforcement

item.title: Iranian attacks on Strait of Hormuz an ‘act of terrorism’, says Adnoc head
item.description: Sultan al-Jaber’s comments come as Donald Trump says US has held talks with Tehran on ending the war

item.title: Gilead nears up to $2bn deal for autoimmune biotech Ouro Medicines
item.description: California-based drugmaker takes advantage of surging share price to strike takeovers after a quiet couple of years

item.title: Starmer warns against counting on ‘quick and early’ end to conflict
item.description: Rachel Reeves to set out plans to support households and businesses after surge in energy prices

item.title: The BBC is a victim of benign neglect
item.description: It is time for a director-general with bold thinking in this age of disruption

item.title: US warns EU to pass trade deal or risk losing ‘favourable’ access to LNG
item.description: European parliament set to vote this week on whether to ratify Turnberry agreement signed last year

item.title: OnlyFans owner Leonid Radvinsky dies at 43
item.description: Ukrainian-American billionaire bought majority stake in controversial porn streaming platform in 2018

item.title: The best tech for tricking out your home office
item.description: A future-proof router, a desktop health-coach device and more

item.title: Goldman Sachs now reckons that oil could take out the 2008 record of $147
item.description: After all, records are made to be broken

item.title: FirstFT: Stocks fall on Trump’s Iran ultimatum
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: LaGuardia closed after collision at airport and European elections latest

item.title: BlackRock’s Fink warns AI may intensify wealth inequality
item.description: CEO of world’s biggest asset manager says rich backers of artificial intelligence could reap most of the rewards

item.title: Berkshire Hathaway to take $1.8bn stake in Tokio Marine
item.description: Investment in non-life insurer marks new Japan venture for Warren Buffett’s conglomerate

item.title: Flights disrupted after crash at NY’s LaGuardia airport kills two people
item.description: Passenger jet flying from Montreal collided with ground vehicle

item.title: Danone to buy meal-in-bottle maker Huel in €1bn deal
item.description: French group swoops on fast-growing UK company to push deeper into ‘complete nutrition’ market

item.title: Nscale’s credit history (slightly chipped)
item.description: Defaults in their start

item.title: Wall Street’s big private capital problem
item.description: Plus, Victory boosts bid for Janus, private capital’s financing wheeze and Giant hits Broadway

item.title: China battery trio gain $70bn as Iran war sparks ‘paradigm shift’
item.description: Share price rises for clean energy companies outstrip oil majors as investors bet on switch to renewables

item.title: Qatar Airways parks long-haul jets in storage in Spain
item.description: Transfer of planes to Teruel airport signals airline is preparing for ongoing Gulf conflict

item.title: The Shakespearean animus of Botín and Orcel
item.description: The Italian’s bid for Commerzbank may prove to be the final act in European banking’s rivalrous drama

item.title: BNP Paribas surges up UK M&A rankings
item.description: French bank boosted by deals including £9.9bn Schroders takeover

item.title: UK home insurers to lose money on underwriting in 2026, analysis predicts
item.description: Higher fuel and energy costs, along with wage and price pressures, will drive up claims costs, EY says

item.title: Oceans take in a lot of heat as Earth’s energy imbalance hits record
item.description: Rate of solar radiation stored in the planet has accelerated over past two decades

item.title: ‘It’s always Putin’ behind every EU migration crisis, official says
item.description: Russian president is ‘biggest driver’ of immigration due to his role in wars over past decade, says EU commissioner

item.title: The ghost bankers haunting the City
item.description: Unable to leave the financial district, some senior staff appear to be working long after handing in their pass
